body_txt: In popular media, butchery has a rather poor reputation. The job of cutting and preparing meat in real life is an undoubtedly messy affair, and few people can stomach working with animal carcasses and gore on a daily basis. Because of this, along with the sheer desensitization towards gore that the job requires, the perceived Lack of Empathy of meat cutters towards animals, and the meat industry's reputation for inhumane treatment towards livestock, butchery came to be stereotyped as a profession only attractive to psychopaths. Thus, the Brutal Butcher was born — a common type of stock villain who seems to enjoy meat cutting a little too much. The Brutal Butcher is usually a Fat Bastard clad in an apron and wielding a sharp, bloodstained cleaver, a butcher's knife , or occasionally a meat hook . More often than not, he (and it's almost always a he) isn't content with butchering mere livestock; he's a violent murderer (if not a Serial Killer outright) who has no qualms about chopping up human victims (or any living being in general) all the same, whether it's for the purpose of making Long Pork pies out of them , satisfying some twisted, cannibalistic hunger , or simply out of sheer sadism (or maybe all of the above). If the latter, they may choose to hang the meats in their windows. Slaughterhouses , abattoirs, and meat lockers are his natural habitat, often lovingly decorated with the remains of butchered livestock and human victims alike as a Corpse-Strewn Lair . For bonus points, he will also be heavily associated with pig imagery —if not an outright Pig Man , in which case part of the horror often comes from the idea of predator and prey switching places . In horror media, this trope is a very common motif for Stock Slashers to the point of becoming cliché. The popularity of this motif derives from how easy it is to draw parallels between the idea of a hulking, implacable serial killer about to gut a helpless victim, and the imagery of a butcher having a frightened pig at their mercy. As a result, many characters may not be literal butchers, but instead just employ the aesthetic. The association between butchery and bloodshed is nothing new. In Latin , the word carnifex literally means "butcher," but it can also be used to describe a hangman, a murderer, or a criminal of low repute. The modern Italian word carnefice is used exclusively to refer to executioners. Compare Evil Chef and Cruella to Animals , two types of villains who share the Brutal Butcher's lack of empathy towards their hapless "ingredients" or soon-to-be mink coats respectively. See also Depraved Dentist for another profession stereotyped negatively in media. Not to be confused with The Butcher , which is about characters with fearsome reputations who are figuratively called such ; this trope covers literal villainous butchers or at least butcher-themed villains (though both can sometimes overlap). Due to the Brutal Butcher's use of meat cleavers, there is a strong overlap between this trope and Brutish Character, Brutish Weapon .
